description: This set of 6 scanning electron microscopy (SEM) micrographs displays the microstructures of an Fe-8.03Mn-6.10Al-0.4C (wt%) low-density duplex steel, processed with two post-hot rolling cooling paths (air cooling, AC; furnace cooling, FC) followed by annealing at 800 °C, 900 °C, and 1000 °C. Subplots (a-c) correspond to AC samples annealed at 800 °C, 900 °C, and 1000 °C respectively, while (d-f) correspond to FC samples at the same annealing temperatures, all with 10 μm scale bars. Identified phases include α-ferrite, α' martensite, δ-ferrite, and γ austenite. AC samples exhibit duplex ferrite-austenite microstructures with needle-like α-ferrite refining austenite grains, while FC samples show banded microstructures with coarser austenite grains and a small fraction of martensite. Increasing annealing temperature reduces α-ferrite content in AC samples and promotes recrystallization in both sample groups. This figure confirms that cooling path and annealing temperature effectively tune the steel's microstructure, which underpins the observed difference in mechanical performance.
